companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: Vera Rubin entered the first hyperscaler production shipment phase in July 2026, with AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, OCI, CoreWeave, Lambda, Nebius, and Nscale among early deployers. NVDA last closed at $194.44 on July 2 (U.S. markets closed July 4 for Independence Day). NVIDIA unveiled a revenue-sharing AI factory model with Sharon AI and Firmus as first partners. Jensen Huang was named a founding member of the UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission, launched July 4.

items.headline: Vera Rubin production shipments begin to hyperscalers in July 2026
items.detail: NVIDIA confirmed Vera Rubin is in full production with first hyperscaler shipments beginning in July 2026. AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, CoreWeave, Lambda, Nebius, and Nscale are among the first deployers. Contract manufacturers Foxconn, Quanta, and Wistron are rolling out systems and racks. Vera Rubin delivers 3.5x training performance over Blackwell Ultra, 5x inference performance, and 10x reduction in cost per inference token, with large-scale Q3 2026 deployments expected.

items.headline: NVIDIA revenue-sharing AI factory model expands — Sharon AI and Firmus are first partners with 40,000 GB300 GPUs
items.detail: NVIDIA is expanding a new revenue-sharing and credit-support business model that aligns economics between NVIDIA and AI cloud providers. Sharon AI is deploying up to 40,000 Grace Blackwell GB300 GPUs under the model alongside Firmus. The approach shifts NVIDIA from hardware vendor to AI factory infrastructure co-investor, deepening long-term deployment relationships and addressing AI cloud company capital constraints beyond one-time chip sales.

items.headline: Jensen Huang named to UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission — inaugural meeting July 8 in Geneva
items.detail: The UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission was announced July 1–2, 2026, with Marc Benioff (Salesforce) and President Paul Kagame of Rwanda as co-chairs. Founding members include Jensen Huang (NVIDIA), Andy Jassy (Amazon), Brad Smith (Microsoft), Jack Clark (Anthropic), and Aidan Gomez (Cohere). The inaugural meeting is scheduled July 8 in Geneva during the ITU AI for Good Global Summit. The commission targets the UN Summit of the Future in September 2026 and will develop shared AI governance standards around infrastructure, health, education, food security, and trust.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 4 — now beyond its June 30 window and past a July 12 Manifold Markets checkpoint (~50% odds). Gemini 3.5 Flash is in production as the interim fast/affordable tier. GOOGL is trading around $357 (July 3 close), up ~4% from its June 29 DJIA inclusion but down ~12% from its May ATH of $408.61. The DeepMind talent exodus (Shazeer to OpenAI, Jumper to Anthropic) continues to be the primary narrative risk for Google's September Siri AI delivery window.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro unreleased on July 4 — Manifold Markets tracking ~50% odds for July 12 checkpoint
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview as of July 4, now more than a week past its self-imposed June 30 window. Google has not confirmed a specific date within July. Manifold Markets prediction markets show approximately 50% odds for a release before July 12. Pricing has been confirmed at approximately $2 input / $12 output per million tokens — well below earlier $15/$60 estimates. The continued delay maintains risk for Apple's September iPhone launch, which relies on the rebuilt Gemini-powered Siri in iOS 27.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash in production; Alphabet joins DJIA June 29 — GOOGL at ~$357
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Flash is in production as the fast/affordable interim model while 3.5 Pro remains in enterprise preview. Alphabet officially entered the Dow Jones Industrial Average on June 29, replacing Verizon, and rose approximately 4% on the inclusion. GOOGL is trading around $357 (July 3 close), down approximately 12% from its May 18 all-time high of $408.61. Q1 2026 Google Cloud revenue was up 63% year over year with a contracted backlog of $460 billion.

items.headline: DeepMind talent exodus: Shazeer to OpenAI, Jumper active at Anthropic — five senior researchers departed
items.detail: Noam Shazeer — Gemini co-lead, VP Engineering, and Transformer paper co-author — departed DeepMind for OpenAI in June 2026. John Jumper (Nobel laureate, AlphaFold2 co-creator) is now officially active at Anthropic, having appeared at the Claude Science launch event on June 30. Three additional senior researchers also departed (Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, Arthur Conmy). The combined exits triggered a $225B single-day market cap loss and remain the primary narrative risk for Google's AI model execution timeline.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft Frontier Co. disclosed its inaugural enterprise partner roster — Unilever, Novo Nordisk, LSEG, Land O'Lakes, and Accenture — three days after its July 2 launch. Microsoft, Anthropic, AWS, and Google co-developed a shared AI jailbreak severity scoring framework announced July 4. Copilot Cowork usage-based billing is in its fourth day. Claude models in Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra are generally available since June 29.

items.headline: Microsoft Frontier Co. partner roster revealed — Unilever, Novo Nordisk, LSEG, Land O'Lakes, Accenture
items.detail: Microsoft's $2.5 billion Frontier Company — launched July 2 with approximately 6,000 embedded engineers — disclosed its inaugural enterprise partner roster: Unilever, Novo Nordisk, London Stock Exchange Group, Land O'Lakes, and Accenture. Led by Rodrigo Kede Lima (former Microsoft Asia president), engineers operate as virtual employees attending standups, writing code, and training citizen developers for six-to-twelve-month engagements. The initiative follows Amazon's $1B FDE program from June 30 in an industry-wide race to embed AI talent with enterprise customers.

items.headline: Microsoft co-develops AI jailbreak severity scoring framework with Anthropic, AWS, and Google
items.detail: Microsoft joined Anthropic, AWS, and Google in co-developing a shared AI jailbreak severity scoring framework under the Glasswing Partners initiative, announced July 4. The framework provides a standardized way to classify, triage, and communicate AI jailbreak risk across providers. It builds on Anthropic's Project Glasswing (which identified 10,000+ critical software vulnerabilities) and extends cross-industry AI security collaboration to include Microsoft's enterprise security posture.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork billing day 4 — evaluating DeepSeek for cost reduction; Claude in Foundry GA on GB300
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing at $0.01 per Copilot Credit is in its fourth day following the July 1 activation. Microsoft is reportedly evaluating DeepSeek V4 as a cost-efficient model within Copilot Cowork alongside Anthropic and OpenAI models to reduce API costs at agentic token scale — scoped to some Cowork workloads, not core Foundry or flagship Copilot products. Claude models (Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5, Opus 4.8) in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs are generally available as of June 29, with a 40% GB300 capacity expansion planned by September.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations on July 4 — day 8 of the limited preview. Broader access is expected in the July 10–17 window. A July 4 report claims OpenAI proposed offering the U.S. government a 5% equity stake modeled on Alaska's Permanent Fund as part of its IPO strategy. The Apple breach-of-contract dispute remains unresolved with outside counsel retained. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share stands at 46.4% — the first time below 50%.

items.headline: OpenAI proposes 5% U.S. government equity stake — modeled on Alaska Permanent Fund
items.detail: OpenAI proposed offering the U.S. government a 5% equity stake valued at approximately $42.6 billion at implied valuation, modeled on Alaska's Permanent Fund dividend structure. The proposal is part of OpenAI's IPO strategy and ties to the planned public offering expected in late 2026 or 2027. No official White House or Treasury response has been reported. The proposal is consistent with OpenAI's broader engagement with the Trump administration and the staged GPT-5.6 deployment under a White House executive order.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna still restricted day 8 — broader access July 10-17; Cerebras deployment this month
items.detail: GPT-5.6 Sol ($5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6) remain in government-staged limited preview, restricted to approximately 20 organizations under a White House executive order requiring staged deployment benchmarking. Sam Altman confirmed broader access around July 10–17. GPT-5.5 remains fully available; GPT-4.5 was retired June 26. OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second for select customers.

items.headline: Apple breach-of-contract dispute unresolved — outside counsel retained; ChatGPT market share 46.4%
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google Gemini. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 4. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. Both sides prefer an out-of-court resolution. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share is 46.4% — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Anthropic launched a Fable 5 Bug Bounty program on HackerOne on July 4 — the first public cybersecurity vulnerability disclosure program for a frontier AI model. Reports indicate Anthropic is actively closing unauthorized Chinese API access routed through Singapore by Ant Financial and ByteDance subsidiaries. Court documents released July 4 reveal a disagreement between Dario Amodei and Pentagon Undersecretary Emil Michael over autonomous weapons and surveillance access. Claude Sonnet 5 is in its fourth day as the default Free/Pro model. Claude Science grants open through July 15. October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target intact.

items.headline: Fable 5 Bug Bounty launched on HackerOne — first public AI vulnerability program for a frontier model
items.detail: Anthropic launched a public Bug Bounty program on HackerOne on July 4 focused on cybersecurity jailbreak vulnerabilities in Fable 5 under controlled conditions. The program is notable as the first public cybersecurity vulnerability disclosure initiative for a frontier AI model with standardized severity scoring and financial rewards. It builds on the Glasswing jailbreak severity framework co-developed with AWS, Microsoft, and Google. Anthropic's security posture has been a central part of its pre-IPO narrative since Project Glasswing identified 10,000+ critical software vulnerabilities in early 2026.

items.headline: Anthropic closing Chinese API access loopholes — Ant Financial and ByteDance Singapore routing targeted
items.detail: The Financial Times reports Anthropic is actively identifying and blocking unauthorized API access by Ant Financial and ByteDance subsidiaries routing Claude API requests through Singapore. Anthropic's method involves monitoring timezone indicators and request relay patterns to detect Chinese-entity access subject to U.S. export controls. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 had their export controls lifted July 1, but Anthropic is maintaining U.S. export law compliance by closing circumvention pathways.

items.headline: Pentagon contract dispute documents released — Amodei vs. Emil Michael over autonomous weapons access
items.detail: Court documents released July 4 reveal a dispute between Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and Pentagon Undersecretary Emil Michael over Anthropic's limits on autonomous weapons guidance and domestic surveillance access via Claude. The disagreement reportedly contributed to the Pentagon migrating two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic models during the Fable 5 ban. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline is rebuilding after the July 1 Fable 5 restoration. No resolution to the underlying policy disagreement has been reported.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Watermelon — the company's next frontier model — is training at scale with GPT-5.5-class performance on current evaluations and uses an order of magnitude more compute than prior Meta models, backed by 600,000+ H100 GPUs. META stock is consolidating near $612 after the 9–10% surge on July 1 following the Meta Compute cloud announcement. Markets are closed July 4. Meta One subscription tiers ($7.99/$19.99) are being tested in Singapore, Guatemala, and Bolivia ahead of a U.S. rollout.

items.headline: Meta Watermelon training update — GPT-5.5-class evals; 600K+ H100s; 'order of magnitude more compute'
items.detail: Business Insider reports Meta's next frontier model (internal codename Watermelon) is achieving GPT-5.5-class performance on current evaluations and uses an order of magnitude more compute than prior Meta models. More than 600,000 H100 GPUs are in production for training, with a 1M GPU cluster planned. Watermelon is designed to be the backbone of Meta's forthcoming consumer and enterprise AI services, including Meta Compute and Meta AI app upgrades. No release date has been announced.

items.headline: Meta Compute cloud analysis deepens — undercutting AWS/Azure by 20-30% using MTIA chips
items.detail: Meta Compute — announced July 1 — is positioning to undercut AWS and Microsoft Azure pricing by 20–30% using Meta's custom MTIA AI accelerators, offering hosted Llama model API access (similar to Bedrock) and raw GPU compute (similar to CoreWeave). Digitimes noted on July 3 that server supply chain contacts see no change to Meta's 2026 shipment commitments. CoreWeave's $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032 is protected. Meta 2026 capex guidance stands at $125–145 billion.

items.headline: Meta One subscription testing in Singapore, Guatemala, Bolivia — $7.99 Plus / $19.99 Premium ahead of U.S. rollout
items.detail: Meta is testing Meta One paid AI subscription tiers — Plus at $7.99/month and Premium at $19.99/month — in initial markets (Singapore, Guatemala, Bolivia) ahead of a planned U.S. rollout, competing directly with ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro (both $20/month). Meta Glasses (launched June 23 at $299–$399 with EssilorLuxottica) show strong early demand. Meta holds 69.2% smart glasses market share. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta under the $60B/6GW multi-generation GPU partnership.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS launched the Secret Cloud for Industry on June 30 — giving defense contractors classified cloud access for the first time with Northrop Grumman as the first partner. The $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework and $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program from AWS Summit DC (June 30) represent the largest single-day federal AI infrastructure commitment in AWS history. GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Amazon Bedrock in the July 10–17 window. Project Rainier runs nearly 500,000 Trainium2 chips with full Claude traffic restored following the July 1 Fable 5 lift.

items.headline: AWS Secret Cloud for Industry gives defense contractors classified cloud access — Northrop Grumman first
items.detail: Amazon Web Services launched AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) — giving U.S. defense contractors classified cloud access for the first time. Northrop Grumman is the inaugural partner. ASCI was announced at AWS Summit Washington DC (June 30) alongside the $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework (ICAMF) for the Intelligence Community and the $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program. CIA Director John Ratcliffe confirmed CIA is using ICAMF with LLMs helping analysts surface hidden intelligence.

items.headline: Bedrock multi-model roster expanding — GPT-5.6 Sol expected July 10-17; full Claude suite restored July 1
items.detail: Amazon Bedrock's multi-model roster continues expanding: Grok 4.3 (xAI) is live with tool calling and streaming; GPT-5.5 and Codex (OpenAI) are generally available; GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Bedrock in the July 10–17 window. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted July 1, restoring the full Anthropic Claude suite on Bedrock. Bedrock AgentCore Web Search is generally available in US East (N. Virginia) with cited real-time web grounding for AI agents.

items.headline: AWS $1B FDE program mirrors Microsoft Frontier Co. — embedded-engineer race defines enterprise AI support
items.detail: Amazon's $1 billion Forward Deployed Engineering organization places thousands of AWS engineers on-site with federal and enterprise customers to co-develop AI solutions, announced June 30 at AWS Summit DC and predating Microsoft's $2.5B Frontier Co. (July 2) by two days. Amazon invested $5B in Anthropic's $65B Series H as a hyperscaler investor. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Anthropic Claude workloads, with 1M+ chips expected by year end under the expanded $100B+/10-year agreement.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameters, V9 architecture, Cursor training data integrated) private beta at SpaceX and Tesla is confirmed as of July 4. A 2T parameter successor is in final training targeting late July. Both Colossus facilities remain committed to third-party tenants. SPCX closed at $157.54 on July 3. The $60B Cursor acquisition proceeds toward Q3 2026 close. xAI plans one new model trained from scratch monthly through 2026.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 private beta confirmed at SpaceX and Tesla — 1.5T params, V9 architecture; 2T model late July
items.detail: July 4 reporting confirms Grok 4.5 — a 1.5 trillion parameter V9 architecture model trained in part on Cursor AI coding data — is running in private beta inside SpaceX and Tesla. Elon Musk claims near-Claude Opus 4.8 performance in internal evaluations; no independent benchmark has been published. A 2 trillion parameter successor is in final training with expected completion late July 2026. xAI plans to release a new model trained from scratch every month for the remainder of 2026.

items.headline: SPCX at $157.54 — both Colossus facilities committed to third parties; Reflection AI $150M/month active
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) closed at $157.54 on July 3 — markets are closed July 4 for Independence Day. The stock peaked at $225.64 on June 16 following the Cursor announcement. Both Colossus 1 (serving Anthropic at approximately $1.25B/month through May 2029) and Colossus 2 (serving Reflection AI at $150M/month through 2029, active since July 1) are fully committed to third-party tenants, raising strategic questions about xAI's dedicated compute access for Grok model training as the 2T model completes.

items.headline: $60B Cursor acquisition on track for Q3 close — SpaceX and Cursor jointly training model for Grok Build
items.detail: SpaceX's $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI parent) — confirmed via SEC Form 8-K, with Cursor having over $2B ARR and more than half the Fortune 500 as clients — is progressing toward a Q3 2026 regulatory close. SpaceX and Cursor are jointly training a new model specifically for Grok Build. Cursor training data is already integrated into Grok 4.5. The combined entity is positioned as a vertically integrated AI coding platform competing with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Google's coding agents.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO is down approximately 26% from its post-Q2 earnings high. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance stands at approximately $16B (48% sequential increase from Q2's $10.8B). Jalapeño — OpenAI's first custom AI inference chip co-developed with Broadcom in nine months — targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment with 1.3 GW compute committed in 2027 and 10 GW through 2029. Six confirmed custom-silicon customers: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and one undisclosed. FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target exceeds $100B.

items.headline: Jalapeño chip targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment — 1.3 GW in 2027, 10 GW through 2029
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom's Jalapeño ASIC — unveiled June 24, developed from design to tape-out in nine months (the fastest ASIC development cycle in high-performance semiconductor history) — targets H2 2026 deployment in Stargate data centers. OpenAI committed 1.3 gigawatts of Jalapeño compute for 2027 with a 10 GW agreement through 2029. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed OpenAI as the sixth custom-silicon customer alongside Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, and one undisclosed. AI semiconductor revenue is now approximately 49% of Broadcom's total revenue.

items.headline: Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B; AVGO down ~26% from high; FY2027 $100B+ target intact
items.detail: Broadcom guided for approximately $16 billion in AI semiconductor revenue in Q3 FY2026 — a 48% sequential increase from Q2's $10.8 billion (+143% YoY). Full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue guidance: $56 billion. FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target: more than $100 billion. AVGO has declined approximately 26% from its post-Q2 earnings high as investors digested maintained rather than raised full-year guidance. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) enables 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute for Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028.

items.headline: Six confirmed custom-silicon customers — Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, and one undisclosed
items.detail: Broadcom now has six confirmed custom-silicon customers for AI accelerator co-development: Google (TPU), Meta (MTIA), Microsoft (Maia), Anthropic (next-gen custom), OpenAI (Jalapeño), and one undisclosed hyperscaler. This breadth positions Broadcom as the dominant ASIC partner for frontier AI compute expansion. VMware Cloud Foundation 9.1 — supporting production AI workloads across AMD, Intel, and NVIDIA — adds a software layer that extends Broadcom's ecosystem reach into enterprise private cloud.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR is trading at approximately $129.30 (July 3 close; markets closed July 4) — up dramatically from the June 25 low of $107.27. CEO Alex Karp characterized frontier AI pricing as a 'wealth tax' on businesses and called the AI industry 'effing insane,' positioning Palantir's AIP with NVIDIA Nemotron as the sovereign, lower-cost government alternative. President Trump's financial disclosure revealed he holds at least $1 million in Palantir shares. Full-year 2026 guidance is raised to approximately 71% revenue growth.

items.headline: Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS launched June 29 — PLTR at ~$129.30; Trump $1M+ stake disclosed
items.detail: The Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System — running Nemotron open-weight models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra for U.S. government agencies — launched June 29 and drove PLTR to an all-time valuation high on July 1. PLTR is trading at approximately $129.30 (July 3 close), up from the June 25 low of $107.27. President Trump's financial disclosure revealed he holds at least $1 million in Palantir shares and recently purchased more. Analyst consensus: 21 Buy, 2 Sell with $143+ average target.

items.headline: Karp calls frontier AI pricing a 'wealth tax' — positions Sovereign AI OS as the enterprise alternative
items.detail: Palantir CEO Alex Karp called the AI industry 'effing insane' in a July 1 CNBC interview, accusing frontier AI firms of overcharging, exploiting customer data, and jeopardizing U.S. national security. Karp positioned Palantir's AIP with NVIDIA Nemotron open-weight models as the answer: agencies own model weights and training data on their own hardware, avoiding per-token frontier API costs. The critique is both competitive positioning and reflects genuine enterprise customer concerns about AI pricing at scale.

items.headline: Army NGC2 advancing to broad deployment; 2026 federal contract wins $11.3B+; Q1 revenue $1.632B
items.detail: The U.S. Army picked Palantir Foundry as the cloud data layer for NGC2 (Next Generation Command and Control), moving from prototype to broad deployment. Major 2026 federal wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services; $1B 5-year DHS AI platform; $300M USDA deal (April); Treasury Department contract (June 23). Q1 2026 revenue was $1.632B (+84.7% YoY). U.S. commercial revenue reached $595M (+133% YoY). Full-year revenue guidance raised to approximately 71% growth. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta continues with Gemini-powered Siri AI on track for public beta mid-July and September 2026 general availability. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay as of July 4 maintains delivery risk for Apple's iPhone launch window. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice. Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option to hedge provider risk. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA clarity.

items.headline: iOS 27 developer beta active — Gemini-powered Siri targets public beta mid-July, GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta has been running since WWDC June 8. The rebuilt Siri is powered by a custom Google Gemini model (~1.2 trillion parameters) through a three-tier routing system: on-device for fast queries, Private Cloud Compute for medium complexity, and Gemini for complex multi-step tasks. Apple pays approximately $1 billion per year for the Gemini license. Public beta is expected mid-July; general availability September 2026. The iOS 27 Extensions framework enabling user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri remains disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act regulatory clarity.

items.headline: OpenAI breach-of-contract notice pending; Apple evaluating Mistral; Gemini delay risk to September launch
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over ChatGPT's perceived lack of prime placement in Siri AI. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 4. Apple is simultaneously evaluating Mistral AI as an additional AI strategy option alongside Gemini, reflecting a multi-provider hedging approach across its 1.5B+ active device base. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay (now beyond the June 30 target) maintains risk for Apple's September iPhone launch timeline that depends on the rebuilt Siri being ready.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is 18 days away (July 22–23 at Moscone Center, San Francisco), where CEO Lisa Su is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6), confirm Helios rack systems, and preview the MI500 series. MI450 samples continue shipping to OpenAI (6GW partnership) and Meta ($60B/6GW). Oracle announced a 50,000-unit MI450 supercluster for Q3 2026. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4. Demand has already exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22-23 at Moscone Center; MI450 formal launch and MI500 preview expected
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 at Moscone Center in San Francisco on July 22–23 is 18 days away. CEO Lisa Su is expected to formally launch the MI450 series accelerators, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6, up to 256 cores), confirm Helios rack-scale systems (3 AI exaflops per rack), and provide a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027 production. Analysts project the MI400 series will generate approximately $7.2 billion in revenue in its first year. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4.

items.headline: Oracle deploys 50,000-unit MI450 supercluster in Q3 2026 — first publicly available MI450-based cluster
items.detail: Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment targeted for Q3 2026. The MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. This joins MI450 commitments from OpenAI (1GW, H2 2026) and Meta (Helios rack shipments H2 2026). AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI and Meta; demand has exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

items.headline: MI500 series: CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E — targeting 1,000x performance vs. MI300X for 2027 production
items.detail: AMD revealed the MI500 series roadmap targeting up to a 1,000x increase in AI performance versus the MI300X (introduced in 2023). The MI500 series uses CDNA 6 architecture on a 2nm process with HBM4E memory. CEO Lisa Su confirmed active customer engagement on MI500 ahead of the Advancing AI 2026 event. AMD denies MI455X delay reports; Helios rack systems remain on track for H2 2026 production. The MI500 series positions AMD to compete against NVIDIA's next-generation platform after Vera Rubin.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity Personal Computer for Windows with a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator is targeting July 2026 — Mac has been GA since May 7. Perplexity for Gov is live via GSA MAS IT at $0.25/agency for 18 months, with FedRAMP compliance underway. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ monthly active users; 92% Fortune 500 usage; $20B valuation from May 2026 round. 2028 IPO target reaffirmed. A fake Perplexity Chrome extension keylogging users has been active since June 29.

items.headline: Perplexity Computer for Windows targeting July 2026 — hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator
items.detail: Perplexity Personal Computer for Windows — featuring a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator that automatically routes tasks between on-device processing (for sensitive data) and frontier cloud models — is targeting July 2026 GA following the Mac launch (GA May 7 for Pro users). The Windows version integrates with Microsoft Office apps and local files, compatible with Intel Core Ultra Series 3 and NVIDIA RTX Spark hardware. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership provides dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters, with Rubin systems rolling in H2 2026.

items.headline: Perplexity for Gov live via GSA MAS IT — $0.25/agency; FedRAMP underway; 92% Fortune 500 usage
items.detail: Perplexity launched 'Perplexity for Gov' alongside the U.S. General Services Administration via the GSA MAS IT schedule at $0.25 per agency for 18 months. Federal users get zero data usage protection and access to ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Claude, and Sonar models. FedRAMP compliance certification is underway. Perplexity counts 92% of Fortune 500 companies as users with partnerships with Samsung, Deutsche Telekom, Vivo, and Airtel. The initiative positions Perplexity alongside Palantir and AWS in the federal AI market.

items.headline: Fake Perplexity Chrome extension keylogging users since June 29; 2028 IPO reaffirmed
items.detail: Microsoft's Defender Security Research Team discovered a malicious Chrome extension called 'Search for perplexity ai' impersonating Perplexity AI while keylogging users' search activity since June 29. Users who installed it need to remove it manually; the threat does not affect Perplexity's core services. CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target, explicitly independent of Anthropic and OpenAI timelines, at a $500M ARR run rate and $20B valuation from its May 2026 financing round.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV is trading at approximately $82.94 (July 3 close; markets closed July 4) — partially recovering from the 14% drop triggered by Bloomberg's July 1 Meta Compute cloud report. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform/$150 citing the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032. Full Anthropic Claude traffic is running on Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters after the July 1 Fable 5 restoration. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 guidance: $12–13B.

items.headline: CRWV at ~$82.94 — partial recovery; Meta take-or-pay protects 2032 revenue; Digitimes supply unchanged
items.detail: CoreWeave shares are trading at approximately $82.94 on July 3, a partial recovery from the $85.68 July 1–2 close following Bloomberg's Meta Compute cloud report. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform with a $150 price target, noting the $21B Meta contract has take-or-pay protections through December 2032 — Meta cannot redirect spend without financial penalty. Digitimes reported on July 3 that server supply chain contacts see no change to Meta's 2026 shipment commitments. The 37-analyst Buy consensus at a $143.41 average target represents a significant discount to current trading levels.

items.headline: Full Anthropic Claude traffic resumed on Vera Rubin NVL72 — Fable 5 restoration clears 19-day backlog
items.detail: Fable 5's global restoration on July 1 cleared the 19-day Claude workload backlog on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters. Full Anthropic inference traffic is now running. ARIA (AI Research & Iteration Agent, built with W&B Weave) entered public preview on June 30. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers, including Anthropic (multi-year deal, April 10), Meta ($21B through 2032), and OpenAI. CoreWeave is among the first cloud providers to deploy Vera Rubin-based instances in 2026.

items.headline: Revenue backlog $99.4B (+50% QoQ); FY2026 guidance $12-13B; NVIDIA ~11% equity stake
items.detail: CoreWeave reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $12–13 billion with an $18–19 billion exit run rate. Revenue backlog surged 50% sequentially to $99.4 billion, anchored by the $21B Meta agreement (through December 2032) and major contracts with Anthropic, Perplexity, and nine of the top ten AI model providers. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. NVIDIA holds approximately 11% equity stake. Jane Street committed approximately $6 billion to CoreWeave's platform.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI announced a joint venture with Bpifrance, UAE sovereign fund MGX, and NVIDIA to develop a 1.4 GW AI campus in the Paris region, with construction beginning H2 2026. The Bruyères-le-Châtel 44MW NVIDIA GB300 facility has not officially opened as of July 4 after missing its end-of-June target; the 40MW baseline has been training models since early 2026. Next model release is projected around July 9 based on the ~71-day release cadence. Equity funding discussions ongoing at approximately €20B valuation for a €3B raise.

items.headline: 1.4 GW AI campus JV — Bpifrance, UAE fund MGX, and NVIDIA; construction beginning H2 2026
items.detail: Mistral AI announced a joint venture with Bpifrance (France's public investment bank), UAE sovereign wealth fund MGX, and NVIDIA to develop a 1.4 gigawatt AI campus in the Paris region, with construction beginning in H2 2026. The campus is the most ambitious European AI infrastructure commitment to date. Mistral's combined European infrastructure target is 200MW by end of 2027, including a €1.2B Sweden investment. The Mistral Compute sovereign GPU cloud, built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems, launched at VivaTech on June 20.

items.headline: Bruyères-le-Châtel 44MW facility still pending official launch; next model release projected ~July 9
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris — 44MW powered by 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs, providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, funded by $830M debt and operated by Eclairion — has not officially launched as of July 4 after missing its end-of-June target. The existing 40MW baseline has been training models since early 2026. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. Based on Mistral's ~71-day release cadence, the next model is projected around July 9.

items.headline: €3B equity raise at ~€20B valuation in discussions; Apple evaluating Mistral for Siri; €1B 2026 revenue target
items.detail: Mistral AI is in discussions to raise approximately €3 billion ($3.5B) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2B), per Bloomberg. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue, up from €200 million in 2025. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri and iOS integration, reflecting Apple's multi-provider hedging strategy amid Gemini 3.5 Pro delivery delays. Industrial launch customers for the Vibe agent platform include Airbus, BMW Group, and ASML. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Tesla capped employee AI tool spending at $200/week effective July 6 — with xAI Grok explicitly exempt — despite internal reports that Tesla employees prefer Anthropic's Claude over Grok. Q2 2026 earnings are scheduled for July 22 at 4:30 PM CT following Q2 deliveries of 480,126, the company's best Q2 ever. TSLA fell 7.49% on July 2 on 'sell-the-news' dynamics. Markets are closed July 4. Optimus Gen 3 V3 production is targeting late July/August at Fremont.

items.headline: Q2 earnings July 22 — following 480,126 delivery beat (18% above consensus); TSLA fell 7.49%
items.detail: Tesla Q2 2026 financial results are scheduled for July 22 at 4:30 PM CT. Q2 deliveries of 480,126 — reported July 2 — beat the company-compiled consensus of 406,024 by 18% and set a new Q2 record (surpassing 466,140 in 2023). TSLA fell 7.49% on July 2 on 'buy the rumor, sell the news' dynamics — its worst day in almost a year. Model 3/Y: 467,762 deliveries; other models (including Cybercab): 12,364. Energy storage: 13.5 GWh deployed. BYD delivered approximately 557,000 pure-EVs in Q2 2026, outpacing Tesla again for the quarter.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 V3 production beginning late July/August — 50,000 unit 2026 goal; Fremont line conversion
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 V3 production is targeting late July/August 2026 at Fremont as the Model S/X line (ended May 2026) converts to robot assembly. The 2026 production goal is 50,000 units, with several thousand Optimus robots already operating inside Tesla's own factories. First external commercial B2B customers are targeted for late 2026. V3 features 22-DoF hands, the AI5 chip (via the Terafab in-house semiconductor fab initiative), and Grok 4.5 voice AI integration. Giga Texas North Campus targets 10M robots per year long-term.

items.headline: Tesla caps employee AI spending at $200/week effective July 6 — Grok exempt; Tesla staff prefer Claude
items.detail: Tesla announced a $200/week cap on employee spending on third-party AI tools, effective July 6, requiring manager sign-off above the threshold. xAI's Grok and Composer are explicitly exempt from the cap, reflecting Tesla's $2 billion investment in xAI's Series E in January 2026. Despite the internal push, multiple sources report that Grok is unpopular among Tesla employees — many preferring Anthropic's Claude for software engineering and knowledge work. The cap applies to competing models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. The Cybercab fleet is at ~60–100+ units across Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area with FSD v14.3 deployed (20% lower inference latency) and a sub-$30K customer price target within six months.

ipoTracker.status: October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; Series H $65B at $965B post-money; ARR $47B; Fable 5 export risk removed July 1; Fable 5 Bug Bounty launched July 4; Pentagon dispute court docs released July 4
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO led by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley with an expected raise of $60B+. The $65B Series H closed at a $965B post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with $15B from hyperscalers including $5B from Amazon, plus Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR: $47B as of May 2026. Fable 5 export controls lifted July 1 removed the largest pre-IPO operational risk that emerged in June. The Fable 5 Bug Bounty program on HackerOne launched July 4 — the first public vulnerability disclosure program for a frontier AI model. Court documents released July 4 reveal a dispute with the Pentagon over autonomous weapons access.
ipoTracker.note: Fable 5 Bug Bounty and Chinese API access enforcement are active pre-IPO security and compliance demonstrations. Pentagon dispute court docs are a new pre-IPO risk factor. October 2026 Nasdaq target remains intact.

latestArticle.title: Fable 5 Bug Bounty launches on HackerOne

ipoTracker.status: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna restricted to ~20 orgs (day 8); broader access expected July 10-17; IPO leaning 2027; 5% government equity stake proposed July 4; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4%
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC in June 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of July 4 (day 8); broader access expected July 10–17. A 5% U.S. government equity stake was proposed July 4, modeled on Alaska's Permanent Fund. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share: 46.4% (Gemini 27.7%, Claude 10.3%).
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company self-disclosure; equity proposal reported, not company-confirmed
ipoTracker.note: July 10–17 broader GPT-5.6 access and Cerebras Sol deployment are the next positive catalysts. Government equity proposal (if real) is a novel pre-IPO development. 2027 IPO posture remains the working assumption.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI proposes 5% U.S. government equity stake

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$157.54 (July 3 close); Grok 4.5 private beta confirmed; 2T model late July; both Colossus committed; $60B Cursor Q3 close on track; CSAM concerns embedded in S-1 ($530M litigation reserve)
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 and pulled back to $157.54 by July 3 (markets closed July 4). Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal started July 1 — both Colossus facilities are now committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 architecture) is in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX as of July 4. A 2T parameter successor is in final training expected late July. The $60B Cursor acquisition (Anysphere) is on track for Q3 2026 close.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Grok 4.5 private beta confirmation is the key July 4 development. Both Colossus facilities commercialized. Cursor Q3 close remains the next major positive catalyst. Adult content and CSAM issues remain unresolved.

latestArticle.title: Grok 4.5 private beta confirmed at SpaceX and Tesla

ipoTracker.status: CBRS public (Nasdaq, May 2026); Q1 2026 revenue $193.4M (+92% YoY); GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment expected this month at 750 tok/sec; $20B+ OpenAI compute deal active
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. Q1 2026 results (released June 23) massively beat estimates: $193.4M revenue vs. $56.65M expected (+92% YoY). Major deals: multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 with Trainium 3 for inference disaggregation. OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment this month is the key near-term positive catalyst. Post-earnings margin guidance selloff remains the near-term stock risk to watch.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

ipoTracker.status: CRWV at ~$82.94 (July 3 close); recovery stalled on Meta cloud competition fears; Evercore Outperform/$150; take-or-pay protections through 2032; Russell 3000 member since June 26; backlog $99.4B
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its Nasdaq IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and trades as CRWV. CRWV dropped 14% on July 1 following Bloomberg's Meta Compute cloud report and is trading at approximately $82.94 on July 3. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform/$150, citing the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032. Russell 3000 inclusion became effective after the June 26 close. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12–13 billion with an $18–19B exit run rate.
ipoTracker.note: Recovery is slower than expected — $82.94 vs. $143 analyst average target. Take-or-pay structure through 2032 provides strong near-term revenue protection regardless of Meta Compute cloud competition.

latestArticle.title: Meta compute plan revives AI bubble worries, but server suppliers stay upbeat

ipoTracker.status: IPO at $42/share; raised $2.1B; $15B market cap; ticker HFCE on Nasdaq; open-source AI infrastructure bellwether
ipoTracker.process: Hugging Face completed its Nasdaq IPO on June 9, 2026, pricing at $42 per share and raising $2.1 billion at a $15 billion market cap under ticker HFCE. The IPO is widely seen as an open-source AI watershed — the first major public listing of a company whose primary assets are open-source model repositories, training infrastructure, and developer community. The stock was trading around $46 per share in mid-June.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 9, 2026
ipoTracker.note: HFCE is a publicly traded open-source AI infrastructure bellwether. Track as an indicator of investor appetite for open-source AI business models.

latestArticle.title: Inside the Hugging Face IPO: What It Means for Open-Source AI in 2026

ipoTracker.stage: Watchlist candidate — CEO explicitly IPO-bound; H2 2026 or 2027 S-1 expected
ipoTracker.status: No filing confirmed; CEO Ali Ghodsi explicitly IPO-bound; $5.4B ARR at 65% YoY growth; $165-175B valuation target
ipoTracker.process: Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi has shifted to an explicitly IPO-bound posture targeting 2027 or H2 2026 at $165–175 billion valuation. Despite calling 2026 'a terrible year to go public' in early June (citing the SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI trifecta absorbing $200B+ in IPO capital), reporting indicates an S-1 filing is still expected in H2 2026. The company has $5.4 billion ARR growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow. SpaceXAI announced native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks at the 2026 Data + AI Summit (June 18).
ipoTracker.confidence: H2 2026 or 2027 S-1 expected per reporting; no filing confirmed
ipoTracker.note: CEO's tone is explicitly IPO-bound. Strong fundamentals support the $165–175B valuation target. Watch for S-1 filing announcement.

latestArticle.title: IPO-bound Databricks reportedly eyes $175B valuation after hitting $5.4B revenue run rate

ipoTracker.stage: Watchlist candidate — no primary-source filing confirmed
ipoTracker.status: Unverified filing claims; no SEC or company confirmation found; $14B+ raised June 2025
ipoTracker.process: Some IPO tracker sites claim Scale AI filed an S-1 in March 2026, but no matching SEC filing, Scale AI announcement, or credible primary-source confirmation has been found as of July 4, 2026. Scale raised more than $14 billion in June 2025 and is unlikely to pursue a near-term IPO given its recent large private financing.
ipoTracker.confidence: Low until verified by primary source
ipoTracker.note: No change from prior checks. No SEC EDGAR record for Scale AI found. Tracker-only claims remain unconfirmed.

latestArticle.title: Can You Invest in Scale AI Pre-IPO?

ipoTracker.stage: High-growth private company — 2028 IPO target confirmed; Apple acquisition talks dead
ipoTracker.status: 2028 IPO reaffirmed; Windows PC targeting July; Perplexity for Gov launched; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; fake Chrome extension security threat active
ipoTracker.process: Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead. CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation from the May 2026 round. Personal Computer GA on Mac since May 7; Windows version with hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator targeting July 2026. Perplexity for Gov launched alongside the GSA with FedRAMP compliance underway. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership uses dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters.
ipoTracker.confidence: No filing; Apple acquisition confirmed dead; 2028 IPO is stated preference
ipoTracker.note: Windows Personal Computer and Perplexity for Gov are the two near-term catalysts this month. Fake Chrome extension is a brand risk worth watching. 2028 IPO timeline is firm.

latestArticle.title: Perplexity 2028 IPO reaffirmed as Anthropic and OpenAI prepare listings

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Colossus 1 serving Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month through May 2029; Colossus 2 serving Reflection AI at $150M/month since July 1; Fable 5 restored — full Claude traffic resumed
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceXAI provides Anthropic access to Colossus 1 at approximately $1.25 billion per month through May 2029 (90-day exit clause). Colossus 1 houses approximately 220,000+ NVIDIA GPUs across 300MW. Colossus 2 began serving Reflection AI on July 1 at $150M/month ($6.3B through 2029), meaning both Colossus facilities are committed to third parties. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted July 1, restoring full global Claude access and enabling resumed normal workload flow to Colossus 1.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed relationship; monthly payment figure from reporting, not company-confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restored July 1 is a direct positive for Colossus 1 utilization. Both Colossus facilities fully committed to third parties raises questions about xAI's own training compute going forward.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX signs $6.3 billion compute deal with Reflection AI for Colossus data center

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $100B+/10-year, 5GW Trainium; Project Rainier active at ~500K chips; Fable 5 restores full Claude on Bedrock; Claude apps gateway on Bedrock; jailbreak framework co-development
partnershipTracker.terms: The April 2026 expanded agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2/3/4 capacity. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads, with 1M+ chips expected by year end. Amazon invested $5B in Anthropic's $65B Series H. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls lifted July 1 restore full Claude suite on Bedrock. The Claude apps gateway (launched July 2) adds a self-hosted enterprise control plane for Claude Code on Bedrock. Microsoft, Anthropic, AWS, and Google co-developed a jailbreak severity scoring framework announced July 4.
partnershipTracker.note: AWS Secret Cloud for Industry expands federal Claude distribution. Jailbreak framework co-development deepens the cross-cloud security partnership. No new contract changes on July 4.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration for up to 5 gigawatts of new compute

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; ~3.5GW TPU capacity starting 2027; Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational; AVGO down ~26% from high — does not affect TPU supply terms
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic's agreement with Google and Broadcom secures approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity expected to start coming online in 2027. Google has invested up to $40 billion in Anthropic. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026 using Broadcom XPUs. Gemini 3.5 Pro delay does not affect the TPU supply relationship.
partnershipTracker.note: AVGO stock decline is market-driven and does not affect partnership terms. TPU capacity comes online in 2027. No structural change to this relationship.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, investment, and GPU optimization
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Claude in Microsoft Foundry GA on GB300 Blackwell Ultra since June 29; Copilot Cowork billing active; Frontier Co. adds enterprise channel for Claude; Maia 200 negotiations ongoing
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic has committed to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity. Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs are generally available as of June 29. Microsoft plans a 40% GB300 capacity expansion by September 2026. Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, 6,000 embedded engineers) adds a new enterprise deployment channel for Claude alongside GPT-5.5. Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active negotiations to deploy Claude on Maia 200 custom AI accelerators (potential 30%+ better performance-per-dollar than NVIDIA for inference). Vera Rubin production shipments to Microsoft Azure began in July 2026.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed; Claude in Foundry GA milestone achieved June 29; Maia 200 talks reported but not finalized
partnershipTracker.note: Frontier Co. adds enterprise professional services deployment channel for Claude on Foundry. Maia 200 negotiations remain the significant pending development. July 4 jailbreak framework co-development confirms ongoing security collaboration.

latestArticle.title: Microsoft, NVIDIA and Anthropic deepen AI collaboration with Claude general availability

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, product distribution, and IP licensing
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed amended partnership; Copilot Cowork billing active day 4; Frontier Co. deepens enterprise channel; GPT-5.6 restricted; broader access expected July 10-17
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner under a non-exclusive IP license through 2032 (amended April 27, 2026). GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 organizations; broader access expected July 10–17. Copilot Cowork billing at $0.01/Copilot Credit entered day 4 following July 1 activation. Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, 6,000 embedded engineers) adds enterprise professional services deployment capacity for OpenAI products. Microsoft is evaluating DeepSeek V4 for some Cowork workloads to reduce API costs.
partnershipTracker.note: Frontier Co. adds enterprise deployment muscle for OpenAI products. July 10–17 GPT-5.6 broader access is the next model availability milestone for this relationship.

latestArticle.title: Microsoft commits $2.5 billion and 6,000 employees to new AI implementation unit

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, and strategic infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; GPT-5.5 and Codex GA on Bedrock; GPT-5.6 expected on Bedrock July 10-17; AWS Secret Cloud for Industry expands federal market; $50B Amazon Stargate investment
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's frontier models (GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4) and Codex are generally available on AWS Bedrock since June 1. GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Bedrock in the July 10–17 window. Amazon invested $50 billion in the March 2026 Stargate funding round. OpenAI holds a 2GW Trainium capacity contract. AWS Summit DC ICAMF and ASCI expand the federal market for OpenAI workloads on Bedrock. The Cerebras-OpenAI $20B+ compute deal adds a third silicon path for OpenAI workloads alongside AWS Trainium.
partnershipTracker.note: AWS Summit DC federal announcements expand the government AI market for OpenAI on Bedrock. GPT-5.6 July 10–17 broader release is the next key Bedrock milestone.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS

partnershipTracker.type: GPU infrastructure and strategic supplier relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 10GW partnership; H2 2026 first-gigawatt Vera Rubin deployment on track; Vera Rubin production shipments to hyperscalers began July 2026
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA agreed to deploy at least 10GW of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI infrastructure, with the first gigawatt targeted for H2 2026 on Vera Rubin. NVIDIA invested $30 billion in Stargate in March 2026. Vera Rubin production shipments began in July 2026 to AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, OCI, and CoreWeave — the infrastructure backbone that will host OpenAI's H2 2026 compute expansion. NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are in production at OpenAI.
partnershipTracker.note: Vera Rubin shipments beginning in July 2026 to hyperscalers advances the H2 2026 first-gigawatt milestone. No structural change to the relationship.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and NVIDIA Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy 10 Gigawatts of NVIDIA Systems

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 6GW partnership beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI; Oracle 50K unit MI450 cluster adds adjacent capacity
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and AMD agreed on a multi-generation 6GW GPU deployment beginning with an initial MI450 rollout of approximately 1GW in H2 2026. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI. Oracle's 50,000-unit MI450 deployment in Q3 2026 adds the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, providing an adjacent cloud resource for OpenAI workloads. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22–23) is expected to formally launch MI450 and provide the next major production milestone update.
partnershipTracker.note: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22–23 is the next major milestone for this relationship — formal MI450 launch and Helios rack confirmation expected.

latestArticle.title: AMD and OpenAI announce strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Stargate Abilene TX flagship (1.2 GW, Oracle Cloud) fully operational; $50B Amazon and $30B NVIDIA Stargate investments committed; Jalapeño ASIC targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's Stargate compute platform spans Oracle, SoftBank, CoreWeave, and related infrastructure partners. The Abilene, TX flagship (1.2 GW on Oracle Cloud) is fully operational. Amazon invested $50 billion and NVIDIA invested $30 billion in the March 2026 Stargate funding round. Jalapeño — OpenAI's first custom ASIC co-developed with Broadcom — targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment with 1.3 GW compute committed for 2027. Financing models, site mix, and partner structures continue to evolve as capacity expands.
partnershipTracker.note: Jalapeño H2 2026 Stargate deployment is the next major structural milestone for this partnership. Treat Stargate as a living infrastructure network rather than a static venture.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño — first custom AI inference chip

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $21B take-or-pay through December 2032; Meta Compute cloud does not affect existing contract; Digitimes confirms 2026 supply commitments unchanged
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave confirmed Meta expanded the relationship to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion, including initial NVIDIA Vera Rubin deployments. The contract is take-or-pay — Meta cannot redirect spend without financial penalty. Meta's announcement of Meta Compute cloud (July 1) raised investor concerns about future CoreWeave utilization, but Digitimes reported on July 3 that server supply chain contacts see no change to Meta's 2026 shipment and revenue commitments. Evercore ISI reiterated that the take-or-pay structure protects 2026 and beyond revenue.
partnershipTracker.note: Take-or-pay protection through 2032 is the key fact distinguishing near-term revenue security from longer-term Meta Compute pricing competition risk.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave and Meta Announce $21 Billion Expanded AI Infrastructure Agreement

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; multi-year agreement; Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters at full utilization following Fable 5 restoration July 1
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave and Anthropic entered a multi-year agreement for Claude workloads at production scale, with infrastructure rolling out in phases beginning in 2026. Financial terms were not disclosed. Following the July 1 Fable 5 restoration, full Anthropic Claude inference traffic is running on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters for the first time since the June 12 export suspension. CoreWeave's first Vera Rubin NVL72 production validation delivered 4x training and 10x inference-per-watt improvements over Blackwell.
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restoration July 1 restored full utilization of CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters for Anthropic. No new contract terms disclosed.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Announces Multi-Year Agreement With Anthropic

partnershipTracker.status: Rebuilding after Fable 5 ban; full Claude restored July 1; Pentagon dispute court docs released July 4 reveal policy disagreement; Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record designation
partnershipTracker.terms: Palantir and Anthropic bring Claude to U.S. government intelligence and defense users inside Palantir products running on AWS infrastructure. The Fable 5 ban (June 12–July 1) disrupted the pipeline, with the Pentagon reportedly migrating two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic models. Court documents released July 4 reveal a dispute between Dario Amodei and Pentagon Undersecretary Emil Michael over autonomous weapons and domestic surveillance limits. Full Fable 5 access was restored July 1. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed partnership; Pentagon dispute details from court documents reported July 4
partnershipTracker.note: Pentagon dispute court docs are a new risk factor for the defense pipeline. The underlying policy disagreement over autonomous weapons access has not been resolved. Full Claude access was restored July 1.

latestArticle.title: Pentagon contract dispute documents released — Amodei vs. Emil Michael

partnershipTracker.type: AI model integration and distribution agreement
partnershipTracker.status: Strained; Apple defaulted to Google Gemini as primary Siri AI; OpenAI retained outside counsel over perceived breach; iOS 27 Extensions framework (multi-AI choice) disabled pending EU DMA
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI signed an integration agreement for ChatGPT in iOS/Siri. At WWDC June 8, Apple announced iOS 27 would feature a fully rebuilt Siri powered by Google Gemini (~$1B/year deal) rather than ChatGPT as the primary AI. The iOS 27 Extensions framework theoretically allows user choice among ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini inside Siri, but remains disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act regulatory clarity. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 4. Both sides prefer an out-of-court resolution.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Original agreement confirmed; breach-of-contract dispute reported, not adjudicated
partnershipTracker.note: This is now a strained partnership rather than an active go-to-market relationship. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (if enabled after EU DMA clarity) would restore ChatGPT distribution inside Siri as a user-selectable option.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI considering legal action against Apple over Siri partnership

partnershipTracker.type: GPU supply and cloud AI infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed ongoing supplier relationship; MI300X powers Azure AI workloads; MI450 evaluation for Azure underway; Vera Rubin production to Azure begins July 2026 (NVIDIA primary)
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD is a GPU supplier to Microsoft Azure for AI workloads, with MI300X and MI450 (when available) powering Azure AI compute capacity alongside NVIDIA hardware. Microsoft is evaluating MI450 for Azure data center deployments in H2 2026. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed active engagement with hyperscalers on MI450 production timelines. The AMD-Microsoft relationship is a supplier-customer partnership distinct from the deeper co-investment structures in Anthropic's or Meta's AMD agreements. NVIDIA remains Microsoft's primary GPU partner for new Azure AI capacity through the Anthropic-Microsoft-NVIDIA strategic alliance.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed ongoing relationship; MI450 Azure evaluation reported but not announced
partnershipTracker.note: This is a supplier-customer relationship rather than a formal strategic co-investment. MI450 evaluation for Azure is the next milestone to watch. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22–23) may clarify Azure deployment terms.

latestArticle.title: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22-23 at Moscone Center

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $60B/6GW multi-generation agreement; MI450 Helios rack shipments in H2 2026; Meta custom MTIA silicon runs alongside AMD for different workloads
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta signed a $60 billion, 6GW multi-generation GPU supply agreement covering MI450 and successor generations. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. Helios rack-scale systems (3 AI exaflops per rack) with MI450 GPUs are targeted for H2 2026 shipments to Meta. Meta's $125–145 billion 2026 capex plan supports both the AMD GPU partnership and Meta's custom MTIA accelerator program (for different workload types). Meta Compute cloud (announced July 1) will leverage both AMD and MTIA compute capacity.
partnershipTracker.note: MI450 Helios rack H2 2026 shipments to Meta are the next major delivery milestone. Meta Compute cloud adds a monetization layer on top of the Meta-AMD infrastructure partnership.

latestArticle.title: AMD CEO confirms MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta

partnershipTracker.type: Strategic equity investment and primary GPU supply relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; NVIDIA holds ~11% equity stake; CoreWeave is primary commercial Vera Rubin deployer; revenue-sharing model expands relationship beyond hardware procurement
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA holds approximately 11% equity stake in CoreWeave (approximately $2 billion in value). CoreWeave purchases NVIDIA hardware at scale — $31 billion capex floor for 2026 alone — and is among the first cloud providers to deploy Vera Rubin NVL72 racks in production. NVIDIA's new revenue-sharing AI factory model (announced July 2) includes CoreWeave as a deployer alongside Sharon AI and Firmus, potentially deepening the economic alignment beyond one-time hardware sales. Vera Rubin production shipments to CoreWeave began in July 2026.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed; equity stake and supply relationship confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: NVIDIA's revenue-sharing model announced July 2 is a new dimension of this relationship — CoreWeave may participate as a revenue-sharing AI factory partner rather than purely a hardware purchaser. This could improve CoreWeave economics at scale.

latestArticle.title: NVIDIA unveils revenue-sharing AI factory model — Sharon AI first deployer with 40,000 GB300 GPUs
